long_description: The communication control computer fails in flight, disrupting radio tuning, data link routing, or communication status reporting.
pilot_actions: Select backup radio paths if available, reduce reliance on data link coordination, use prebriefed comm procedures, and recover if communication loss compromises mission safety.

long_description: The communication control computer mismanages antenna or channel switching in flight, causing intermittent transmit or receive loss across assigned radios.
pilot_actions: Reattempt essential calls on alternate radios or presets, simplify communication setup, and use backup procedures if primary routing remains unreliable.

long_description: Data-link control through the communication computer is lost in flight, degrading tactical coordination, remote cueing, or network track exchange.
pilot_actions: Fall back to voice procedures, simplify formation tactics, and treat offboard tactical data as unavailable until stable communications are restored.

long_description: Communication crypto or secure-link synchronization fails in flight, preventing secure exchange even though radios remain powered.
pilot_actions: Switch to authorized alternate communication modes or procedures, reduce dependency on secure networking, and terminate tasks that require protected coordination.

long_description: Radio tuning logic behaves incorrectly in flight, selecting wrong presets, frequencies, or channel states and causing communication confusion.
pilot_actions: Cross-check active frequency carefully, minimize channel changes, and use prebriefed backup frequencies if tuning remains suspect.
